description: The optional ai-harness Cognee deploy now boots and passes /health with the local image-digest and proxy-healthcheck fixes, but dataset cognify and /api/v1/search still fail end-to-end. Live logs show repeated 'LLM connection test timed out after 30s' during cognify, and direct OpenAI completion checks with the currently injected LLM_API_KEY return quota/billing errors for valid models. Compute-stack's shared Cognee instance shows the same external 504 behavior on /api/v1/search, so this is now an operator-credential/provider readiness blocker rather than only a deploy-shape bug. Resolve the funded provider/credential path (or supported alternate provider) and verify upload + cognify + query succeed against ai-harness-cognee.apps.compute.lan.
title: Restore Cognee queryability with funded LLM access

acceptance_criteria: Phase 2 has clear child tasks, GSD phase docs reference the active work, and the phase closes only after verification passes across the self-hosted workflow.

description: Finish CORE-05 by making ai-harness the opinionated Beads + GSD + Codex/OpenCode foundation for new and existing repos. This phase should lock the product doctrine, harden curated cleanup for non-harness AI workflow droppings, strengthen adoption and doctor coverage, and validate the dogfood loop on this repository plus sample repos.
title: Phase 2: self-hosted harness rollout

acceptance_criteria: The doctrine clearly states what ai-harness installs, what it removes, what it preserves, and which files are the source of truth for dogfooded updates.
close_reason: Verified: doctrine now defines the installed foundation, preserve/remove contract, and source-of-truth rules in README.md, docs/ai-harness-premise.md, and .planning/PROJECT.md
description: Write down the opinionated product doctrine for ai-harness: Beads, GSD, Codex, and OpenCode are the foundation; src/templates/** is canonical; existing repos preserve user files by default; and non-harness AI workflow droppings are only removed through curated manifests. Update the project and planning docs so later cleanup and doctor work has a stable contract to follow.
title: Codify ai-harness doctrine and cleanup contract

acceptance_criteria: Curated manifest entries cover the known legacy droppings we want removed, and tests prove they are removed only when explicitly requested.
close_reason: Verified: legacy-ai-frameworks-v1 now covers deprecated .planning/TRACEABILITY.md and cleanup behavior passes unit, integration, typecheck, full test, and dist smoke validation
description: Inventory the non-harness AI workflow leftovers we already know how to reason about, then extend curated cleanup manifests so existing-repo adoption can remove them safely. Keep the scope explicit: legacy wrappers, duplicate planning systems, deprecated assistant runtime files, and other known artifacts that conflict with the ai-harness foundation.
title: Expand curated cleanup manifests for legacy AI workflow droppings

acceptance_criteria: The test matrix covers representative clean, legacy, and mixed states so future adoption changes can be made without guessing.

description: Add integration coverage for the adoption states we care about most: clean repos, repos with older ai-harness/scaiff artifacts, repos with mixed custom AI files, and repos with conflicting workflow leftovers. Use these fixtures to prove preserve-by-default behavior and curated cleanup behavior together.
title: Build adoption fixture matrix for mixed repository states

acceptance_criteria: Doctor reports deprecated known artifacts with actionable guidance without encouraging broad heuristic deletion of user files.

acceptance_criteria: The self-hosted repo and sample adoption targets validate cleanly, with any newly discovered work filed as linked follow-ups before Phase 2 closes.

description: Follow up on the remaining gap between the desired autonomous GSD workflow integration and the current ai-harness scaffold. Decide whether ai-harness should scaffold a dedicated autonomous workflow doc, how that should coexist with AGENTS.md and .codex docs, and whether post-bd-init config should enable JSONL or event export for automation.
title: Decide autonomous.md and Beads config integration strategy

description: Capture the new optional Cognee deployment findings in repo guidance: use the published image digest instead of the missing v0.5.6 Docker Hub tag, probe /health in kamal-proxy, set a supported LLM_MODEL explicitly, and require post-deploy upload/cognify/query verification plus operator-ready LLM credentials with quota. Map the follow-up into GSD artifacts so future work can resume without repeating the live debugging session.
title: Document Cognee preflight and post-deploy verification

description: Cognee app container now boots far enough to run migrations, but startup still fails behind kamal-proxy. Current logs show Alembic migration failure on ALTER TYPE pipelinerunstatus ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS because the enum type does not exist, followed by a runtime configuration error: selected vector database provider pgvector with vector dataset handler lancedb requires ENABLE_BACKEND_ACCESS_CONTROL=false or a supported handler alignment. Resolve the migration/runtime mismatch so the app becomes healthy on ai-harness-cognee.apps.compute.lan.
title: Fix Cognee boot migration/runtime mismatch

Cover graceful degradation when bd or .beads is unavailable, phase-level issue tracking expectations, verification-before-close rules, and landing/bootstrap guidance. Note the current gap that no autonomous.md exists in this repo and avoid hard-coding unsupported Beads config keys without version validation.
title: Harmonize Beads and GSD workflow guidance
